title: Braised German Cabbage Casserole
description: A hearty, comforting German-inspired casserole featuring tender cabbage and rich sauce. Perfect for a cold winter night.
instructions: In a large pan, cook the ground pork over medium-high heat until browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Add the chopped onions and minced garlic to the pan and cook until the onions are translucent.
Add the shredded cabbage to the pan and cook until it starts to soften.
Sprinkle the caraway seeds over the cabbage mixture and cook for 1 minute.
In a small bowl, mix together the flour and a splash of braised pork stock to create a slurry.
Add the slurry to the pan and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 5 minutes, or until the cabbage is tender.
Transfer the cabbage mixture to a 9x13 inch baking dish.
Top the cabbage mixture with the breadcrumbs and dot the top with butter.
Cover the dish with aluminum foil and bake for 25 minutes.
Remove the foil and continue baking for an additional 10-15 minutes, or until the top is golden brown.
